category_title: Students with ASD who exhibit school avoidance or distress during schedule changes
scenario_title: Adjusting to an unexpected school assembly
minimal_context: The student is preparing to transition to an unexpected school assembly in the high school setting.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: There is a change in our class schedule today. We are going to a school assembly instead of our regular class.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: But my agenda says it is time for regular class. I want to follow the routine and consistency.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: The schedule is different today. We need to go to the assembly.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I can’t handle that change in schedule. I just don't want to go.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We have to go out into the hallway and head to the assembly now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Begins flapping hands and spinning) It is too chaotic and noisy out there! I am having a difficult time adjusting!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: There is a change in our class schedule today. We are going to a school assembly instead of our regular class.

STUDENT: But my agenda says it is time for regular class. I want to follow the routine and consistency.

TUTOR: The schedule is different today. We need to go to the assembly.

STUDENT: I can’t handle that change in schedule. I just don't want to go.

TUTOR: We have to go out into the hallway and head to the assembly now.

STUDENT: (Begins flapping hands and spinning) It is too chaotic and noisy out there! I am having a difficult time adjusting!

task.content: Responding to unexpected changes in the daily high school schedule, specifically delayed openings, fire drills, or assemblies

context.environment: chaotic and often noisy secondary environment
context.materials: student's agenda or folder, paper, Apple iOS devices with visual applications (apps) such as pictello and iPrompts

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When there was a change in the class schedule (e.g., school assembly or fire drill)
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: had a difficult time adjusting, overt repetitive physical behaviors (e.g., flapping or spinning)

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: they [one student with ASD] just don't come
behavior_patterns.internal_state: they can't handle that change in schedule
related_quotes: when we have delayed opening, they [one student with ASD] just don’t come because they can’t handle that change in schedule.
